title: Performance Optimization
content:
### Performance Optimization React is performant by default, but certain patterns can cause unnecessary re-renders, slow loading, or jank. Optimize based on measured issues, not assumptions. --- ### Common Performance Issues | Issue | Cause | Solution | |-------|-------|----------| | Unnecessary re-renders | Parent re-renders trigger children | React.memo, useMemo, useCallback | | Large bundle size | Too many components imported | Code splitting, lazy loading | | Expensive computations | Heavy calculations on every render | useMemo | | Large lists | Rendering thousands of items | Windowing (react-window) | ### React.memo ```jsx import { memo } from 'react'; const ExpensiveList = memo(function ExpensiveList({ items }) { return items.map(item => ); }); ``` ### Windowing with react-window ```jsx import { FixedSizeList } from 'react-window'; function VirtualList({ items }) { const Row = ({ index, style }) => (
{items[index].name}
); return ( {Row} ); } ``` ### Image Optimization ```jsx // Lazy load images // Use modern formats ``` ### Measuring Performance | Tool | Use | |------|-----| | React DevTools Profiler | Component render timing | | Chrome Performance Tab | Overall page performance | | Lighthouse | Audits and scoring | | Web Vitals | Real user metrics (LCP, FID, CLS) | ### Optimization Checklist - [ ] Use production builds - [ ] Implement code splitting by routes - [ ] Memoize expensive calculations with useMemo - [ ] Wrap stable components with React.memo - [ ] Use windowing for long lists - [ ] Optimize images (lazy loading, modern formats) - [ ] Debounce search inputs - [ ] Avoid unnecessary state updates > **Key Point:** Do not optimize prematurely. Measure first, then optimize the actual bottlenecks. The React 19 compiler reduces the need for manual memoization. Focus on code splitting and bundle size for the biggest gains.

quiz.question: What tool helps identify unnecessary re-renders?
When should you use React.memo?
options: When profiling shows a component re-renders with the same props
